question: What is the role of antimicrobial peptide genes in transgenic crop protection?
options: Introducing AMP genes into crops provides enhanced resistance to fungal and bacterial pathogens without chemical pesticides
AMP genes only function in wild plants
Transgenic crops never express AMPs effectively
explanation: Transgenic expression of plant or animal AMP genes (defensins, cecropins, thionins) in crops like rice, wheat, and tobacco enhances resistance to major fungal and bacterial diseases.
